summary: This week's AI news highlights significant advancements in AI technology, including new models and partnerships. OpenAI and Hugging Face addressed a security incident, while Google released new Gemini models. NVIDIA announced advancements in graphics and simulation with Agentic and Physical AI.
insight: The increasing use of AI in various industries, such as healthcare and finance, raises concerns about data privacy and security, emphasizing the need for robust safeguards and regulations.
highlights: OpenAI and Hugging Face partner to address security incident during model evaluation
Google releases new Gemini models, including Gemini 3.6 Flash and 3.5 Flash-Lite
NVIDIA advances graphics and simulation with Agentic and Physical AI

articles.title: Google releases three new Gemini models — but no 3.5 Pro
articles.description: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.

articles.title: Introducing the ChatGPT for small business program
articles.description: OpenAI launches the ChatGPT for Small Businesses program, helping entrepreneurs build AI skills, automate work, and grow with ChatGPT Work.

articles.description: We’re introducing new Gemini models, including Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite and 3.5 Flash Cyber.

articles.title: OpenAI and Hugging Face partner to address security incident during model evaluation
articles.description: OpenAI and Hugging Face share early findings from a security incident during AI model evaluation, highlighting advanced cyber capabilities and lessons for defenders.

articles.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
articles.description: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.
